title: Chicken Mozzarella Bake (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Chicken Mozzarella Bake is a simple, cheesy high-protein dinner with chicken, marinara, and melted mozzarella. It is easy to portion and ideal for meal prep and Eati logging.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Marinara (tomato sauce)
- Mozzarella
- Garlic
- Italian seasoning
- Herbs
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Preheat oven to 400F (200C).
2. Season chicken and place in a baking dish.
3. Add marinara and mix with garlic and Italian seasoning.
4. Top with mozzarella and herbs.
5. Bake until chicken is cooked and cheese melts; serve and log in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 395 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 18g
Protein: 50g

Tip: portion cheese carefully if you are watching fat calories.

sections.heading: Why Chicken Mozzarella Bake Is Ideal for Fat Loss
sections.content:
At 395 calories and 50g of protein per serving, this dish ranks among the most efficient high-protein dinners you can make. For comparison, restaurant-style chicken parmesan easily hits 900–1,100 calories per serving — this recipe delivers similar satisfaction at less than half the calorie cost.

Why it works for weight loss:
• Protein-to-calorie ratio: Over 12g protein per 100 calories — elite by any measure.
• No breading = lighter meal: Skipping the fried breadcrumb crust saves 150–250 calories while keeping the Italian-comfort-food vibe.
• Marinara provides volume: Tomato sauce adds flavor and minerals with minimal calories (~30 cal per 1/4 cup).
• High satiety: Lean protein + tomato's water content + melted cheese keeps you full 4–5 hours.
• Kid-friendly and family-friendly: The familiar flavor profile means the whole household will eat it.

This recipe is an anchor of many successful meal-prep rotations. Check your daily protein needs with a protein calculator.

sections.heading: Chicken Mozzarella Bake vs Chicken Parmesan: Which Is Better?
sections.content:
If you love Italian-American flavors but want to stay in a calorie deficit, understanding the difference matters:

Classic Chicken Parmesan:
• Calories: 850–1,100 per serving
• Protein: 45–55g
• Fat: 40–55g
• Main issue: breading + frying adds 300–400 calories per serving.

This Chicken Mozzarella Bake:
• Calories: 395 per serving
• Protein: 50g
• Fat: 18g
• Advantage: identical flavor profile (marinara + mozzarella + Italian herbs) without the breading.

When to choose which:
• Fat loss / moderate calorie day → chicken mozzarella bake every time.
• Carb/cal flexibility day → chicken parmesan, with half portion of pasta.
• Meal prep → chicken mozzarella bake wins because it reheats better (no soggy breading).
• Kid approval → chicken parmesan often wins, but many kids accept the bake if sauce is flavorful.

For more on managing Italian-style meals, see [how to eat out and still lose weight](/blog/how-to-eat-out-and-still-lose-weight) and [how to lose weight without giving up your favorite foods](/blog/how-to-lose-weight-without-giving-up-your-favorite-foods).

sections.heading: Marinara Sauce: Quality Matters More Than You Think
sections.content:
The jarred marinara you choose can swing this recipe by 80+ calories per serving and dramatically affect flavor.

What to look for:
• No added sugar — cheap brands add 6–10g sugar per 1/2 cup. Pick brands with under 5g sugar.
• Olive oil as fat source — not soybean, canola, or 'vegetable oil' blends.
• Short ingredient list — tomatoes, olive oil, onion, garlic, salt, herbs. Nothing else needed.
• Low sodium — quality brands run 300–400mg per 1/2 cup; cheap brands hit 600mg+.

Recommended brands:
• Rao's Homemade Marinara (premium, excellent flavor)
• Victoria Marinara (close second, cheaper)
• Mezzetta Napa Valley Marinara
• ALDI's Specially Selected Tomato Basil (budget pick)

Homemade option: Simmer 1 can crushed tomatoes + 2 garlic cloves + 1 tbsp olive oil + 1 tsp Italian herbs for 20 minutes. Cheaper, better, and about 30 calories per 1/2 cup.

Calorie math: 1 cup marinara across 4 servings = 30 cal per serving (from this recipe). Use 2 cups for a saucier version — still only 60 cal per serving.

sections.heading: What to Serve With Chicken Mozzarella Bake
sections.content:
Choose a side based on your daily calorie target:

Lower-calorie (under 150 cal total):
• Mixed green salad with light Italian vinaigrette
• Roasted zucchini and yellow squash
• Steamed or sautéed spinach with garlic
• Roasted Brussels sprouts
• Cauliflower rice with parmesan and herbs

Moderate-carb (150–250 cal):
• 1/2 cup whole-wheat pasta tossed with a splash of marinara
• 1/2 cup farro or quinoa
• 1 medium baked potato
• Whole-grain garlic bread (1 small slice)

Classic Italian sides:
• Caprese salad (tomato + mozzarella + basil) — ~150 cal, adds another 5g protein
• Sautéed broccoli rabe with olive oil and garlic
• Zucchini noodles tossed with marinara

Balanced Italian plate:
1 serving chicken mozzarella bake + 1.5 cups spiralized zucchini with marinara + a simple green salad = ~500 calories with 55g protein. Keeps you satisfied and firmly in a deficit. See [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss) for more sides.

sections.heading: Meal Prep, Freezing, and Kid-Friendly Variations
sections.content:
Few recipes prep and freeze as well as this one.

Batch cooking (8 servings):
1. Arrange 8 chicken breasts in two 9×13 baking dishes.
2. Top each with 2–3 tbsp marinara.
3. Add herbs, garlic, and 1 oz mozzarella per breast.
4. Bake uncovered at 400°F for 22–25 min or until internal temp hits 165°F.

Storage:
• Fridge: 4 days in airtight containers.
• Freezer: Up to 3 months. Wrap individually for easy single-serving thaws.

Reheating:
• Oven (best): 325°F for 15 min. Keeps cheese and chicken both perfect.
• Microwave: 90 seconds covered, stir sauce, 45 more seconds.

Kid-friendly variations:
• Cut chicken into strips before baking — feels more like nuggets.
• Add pepperoni slices on top for a pizza-style version (adds ~50 cal).
• Use half mozzarella, half shredded parmesan for a saltier kick kids love.
• Serve over buttered whole-wheat spaghetti for a full Italian dinner.

Low-sodium variation: Use no-salt-added marinara, fresh mozzarella (lower sodium than shredded), and skip added salt. Save 400–600mg sodium per serving.

Log all variations in Eati to confirm macros hit your target. Learn tracking tips in [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly).

conclusion: A comfort-style bake that still supports a high-protein fat loss routine. Use Eati to confirm your serving size and macros.
metaTitle: Chicken Mozzarella Bake Recipe: 50g Protein in 395 Calories (No Breading)
metaDescription: Lighter than chicken parmesan — this baked chicken mozzarella with marinara delivers 50g protein in 395 calories per serving. Includes macros, side pairings, marinara tips, and meal prep guide.

faqs.question: How many calories in chicken mozzarella bake?
faqs.answer: Approximately 395 calories per serving with 50g of protein and 18g of fat, using 6 oz chicken breast, 1/4 cup marinara, and 1 oz mozzarella. Using part-skim mozzarella or reducing cheese by 25% can drop this to ~345 calories per serving.

faqs.question: Is chicken mozzarella bake healthy?
faqs.answer: Yes. It's one of the most protein-efficient dinners you can make: 50g of protein in under 400 calories with no breading or frying. The marinara adds vegetables (tomato), and mozzarella provides calcium. It fits into most fat-loss, muscle-building, and family-friendly meal plans.

faqs.question: How is this different from chicken parmesan?
faqs.answer: Chicken parmesan uses a breadcrumb breading and is typically pan-fried before baking, adding 300–400 calories per serving. This mozzarella bake skips both steps — same Italian flavor (marinara + melted cheese) but roughly half the total calories.

faqs.question: Can I freeze chicken mozzarella bake?
faqs.answer: Yes, it freezes exceptionally well. Cool completely, wrap individual portions tightly in plastic + foil, and freeze up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the fridge and reheat in a 325°F oven for 15 minutes for the best texture.

faqs.question: What's the best pasta to serve with chicken mozzarella bake?
faqs.answer: For lower calories, use zucchini noodles or spaghetti squash (~30–40 cal per cup). For a balanced meal, 1/2 cup whole-wheat spaghetti or penne (~100 cal) tossed with a splash of marinara. Stay under 3/4 cup pasta to keep the meal in a moderate fat-loss range.
